paper_metadata.title: Decision Transformer: Reinforcement Learning via Sequence Modeling
paper_metadata.abstract: We introduce a framework that abstracts Reinforcement Learning (RL) as a sequence modeling problem. This allows us to draw upon the simplicity and scalability of the Transformer architecture, and associated advances in language modeling such as GPT-x and BERT. In particular, we present Decision Transformer, an architecture that casts the problem of RL as conditional sequence modeling. Unlike prior approaches to RL that fit value functions or compute policy gradients, Decision Transformer simply outputs the optimal actions by leveraging a causally masked Transformer. By conditioning an autoregressive model on the desired return (reward), past states, and actions, our Decision Transformer model can generate future actions that achieve the desired return. Despite its simplicity, Decision Transformer matches or exceeds the performance of state-of-the-art model-free offline RL baselines on Atari, OpenAI Gym, and Key-to-Door tasks.

extraction_data.innovation: Decision Transformer models trajectories autoregressively using a transformer architecture, replacing traditional reinforcement learning methods with sequence modeling. It uses return-to-go as a conditioning signal to enable future reward-based action generation.
extraction_data.pretraining_corpus: Offline reinforcement learning datasets (Atari, OpenAI Gym, Key-to-Door)
extraction_data.extension: Uses return-to-go instead of rewards as input, enabling the model to generate actions based on desired future performance rather than past rewards.
extraction_data.application: Offline reinforcement learning, policy generation, sequence modeling for decision making
extraction_data.paper_title: Decision Transformer: Reinforcement Learning via Sequence Modeling

extraction_data.innovation: Decision Transformer frames reinforcement learning as a sequence modeling problem, using a transformer decoder to predict actions conditioned on return-to-go, state, and action sequences, enabling end-to-end learning without explicit policy or value functions.
extraction_data.pretraining_corpus: OpenAI Gym environments, Graph Shortest Path dataset
extraction_data.application: Reinforcement learning, decision making, control tasks
extraction_data.extension: Uses return-to-go conditioning to guide action generation, enabling zero-shot task transfer and goal-conditioned behavior.
extraction_data.paper_title: Decision Transformer: Reinforcement Learning via Sequence Modeling
